sources: B.Com(Hons) Technical Analysis - Dr. Kanu Jain
The Complete Guide to Trading - CFI
The ART of Trading - Bennett McDowell

head_and_shoulders.name: Head and Shoulders Top
formation: Three peaks: left shoulder, higher head, right shoulder
Neckline connects lows between shoulders and head
Volume typically highest on left shoulder, declining on head and right shoulder

entry_rules.condition: Enter short on neckline break with volume confirmation
entry_rules.confirmation: Close below neckline on increased volume

exit_rules.condition: Target = neckline to head distance projected below neckline

inverted_head_and_shoulders.name: Inverted Head and Shoulders
formation: Three troughs: left shoulder, lower head, right shoulder
Neckline connects highs between shoulders and head
Forms at end of downtrend

entry_rules.condition: Enter long on neckline breakout with volume
entry_rules.confirmation: Close above neckline on increased volume

exit_rules.condition: Target = neckline to head distance projected above neckline

double_top.name: Double Top
formation: Two peaks at similar price level forming M shape
Valley (neckline) between the two peaks
Second peak on declining volume signals weakening buyers

entry_rules.condition: Enter short on break below valley/neckline

exit_rules.target: Measured move = peak to neckline distance

double_bottom.name: Double Bottom
formation: Two troughs at similar price level forming W shape
Peak between troughs acts as resistance
Appears at end of bearish trend

entry_rules.condition: Enter long on break above resistance between troughs

exit_rules.target: Measured move = trough to peak distance

ascending_triangle.name: Ascending Triangle
formation: Flat resistance line at top
Rising support line (higher lows)
Price compresses toward apex

entry_rules.condition: Enter long on breakout above flat resistance

exit_rules.target: Height of triangle projected from breakout

confirmation_signals: Volume declining during formation then expanding on break

descending_triangle.name: Descending Triangle
formation: Flat support at bottom
Declining resistance line (lower highs)
Bearish bias until broken

entry_rules.condition: Enter short on breakdown below flat support

symmetric_triangle.name: Symmetric Triangle
formation: Converging trendlines: lower highs and higher lows
Each peak lower than previous, each bottom higher than previous
Breakout direction follows prior trend

entry_rules.condition: Enter in direction of breakout from apex
entry_rules.confirmation: Wait for close outside triangle with volume

exit_rules.target: Widest part of triangle projected from breakout

bull_flag.name: Bull Flag
formation: Sharp rally (flagpole) followed by rectangular or parallelogram consolidation
Consolidation slopes slightly against trend
Volume declines during flag, expands on breakout

entry_rules.condition: Enter long on breakout above flag upper boundary

bear_flag.name: Bear Flag
formation: Sharp decline (flagpole) followed by upward-sloping consolidation
Brief pause before continuation lower

entry_rules.condition: Enter short on breakdown below flag lower boundary

breakaway_gap.name: Breakaway Gap
formation: Price gaps out of congestion/trading range
Requires enthusiasm: many more buyers (up) or sellers (down)
Marks start of new trend leg

entry_rules.condition: Enter in gap direction on first pullback to gap edge

exit_rules.stop: Below gap low (bullish) or above gap high (bearish)

runaway_gap.name: Runaway (Measuring) Gap
formation: Gap mid-trend caused by increased interest
Represents panic buying (up) or panic selling (down)
Trend continues in gap direction until filled

entry_rules.condition: Add to position in trend direction; gap acts as support/resistance

exit_rules.target: Equal distance from prior swing to gap, projected forward

exhaustion_gap.name: Exhaustion Gap
formation: Occurs near end of extended trend
High volume and large price difference from prior close
Often first signal of trend exhaustion

entry_rules.condition: Fade the gap direction after confirmation of reversal
entry_rules.confirmation: Distinguish from runaway gap via exceptionally high volume

cup_and_handle.name: Cup and Handle
formation: Cup: rounded bottom after advance (bowl shape)
Handle: small consolidation on right side of cup
Breakout above handle signals continuation

entry_rules.condition: Enter on breakout above handle resistance

confirmation_signals: Handle drifts down on low volume

rounding_bottom.name: Rounding Bottom (Saucer)
formation: Long-term gradual transition from bearish to bullish bias
Best identified on weekly charts
Volume typically lowest at bottom, increases on right side

entry_rules.condition: Enter on break above saucer rim / resistance

pin_bar.name: Pin Bar
formation: Long wick (tail) rejecting a price level
Small body at opposite end of wick
Bullish pin: long lower wick at support; Bearish pin: long upper wick at resistance

entry_rules.condition: Enter on close above pin bar high (bullish) or below pin low (bearish)

support.definition: Price level where demand is strong enough to prevent further decline
support.logic: As price declines toward support, buyers become more inclined to buy and sellers less inclined to sell
trading_rules: Buy at support with stop below level

resistance.definition: Price level where selling is strong enough to prevent further rise
resistance.logic: As price advances toward resistance, sellers become more inclined to sell
trading_rules: Sell or short at resistance with stop above level
